note: V13 future-timestamp drift cap finalised at 30 s (not 10 s as previously committed). (A) include/sost/params.h:857 — max_future_drift_at(height) returns 30 for height >= V13_HEIGHT instead of 10. Comment block at line 268 updated; the V13 fork-notes block at line 811 rewritten to capture the 10 s evaluated-and-rejected rationale (real-world clock skew + NTP cycle jitter + network latency between honest miner and validator can plausibly reach 5-10 s in honest setups, which would have produced legitimate-block rejection for no meaningful attack-defense gain — the cASERT avg288 controller absorbs any sub-block drift regardless of whether the cap is 10 or 30). (B) tests/test_v13_helpers.cpp — 6 static_asserts + 3 runtime TEST() assertions updated from 10 to 30; descriptive comments rewritten. Compile + run: 25/25 PASS on the deployment host. (C) tests/test_v13_drift_fork.cpp — V13 boundary tests at h=12000 updated: accepts now=+30 (boundary), rejects now=+31 (off-by-one), still rejects now=+60 (pre-V13 cap NOT in effect, 60>30), added new assertion 'h=12000 accepts ts=now+20 (honest-miner tolerance within 30s cap)'. Cross-regime monotonicity test cap_at_v13==30 and cap_far_future==30. Compile + run: 27/27 PASS. (D) Docs sweep: docs/V13_RELEASE_CANDIDATE.md, docs/V13_MINER_OPERATOR_CHECKLIST.md, docs/V13_ACTIVATION_PLAN.md, docs/V13_PUBLIC_SCOPE_UPDATE.md, docs/V13_SPEC.md, docs/V13_COOLDOWN_AUDIT.md, docs/V13_BEACON_II_B_III_GAPS.md, docs/V13_POPC_GOLDVAULT_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, website/sost-explorer.html, website/sost-whitepaper.html, website/sost-protocol-spec.html, website/casert-spec.html — all 12 files now consistently say '60 s -> 30 s' / '30 s drift cap' / 'NTP strongly recommended' (no more 'NTP MANDATORY' wording anywhere). Miner-checklist section header changed from '## C. NTP (MANDATORY post-V13)' to '## C. NTP (strongly recommended post-V13)'. Operator-rationale paragraph added wherever the V13 spec is summarised: '30 seconds materially limits future-timestamp manipulation while preserving operational tolerance for honest miners'. The 10 s figure remains in historical comparison contexts (e.g. 'evaluated and rejected') so the trajectory is auditable. (E) website/api/explorer_version.json bumped v275 -> v276. ZERO consensus rule change beyond the drift-cap constant. ZERO change to V13_HEIGHT, cASERT, lottery_exclusion_window_at, Beacon Phase II-A activation, or any other V13-confirmed item. ZERO change to Bitget/CEX dossiers (those are external listing artefacts, not the protocol).
